records.title: Frequently Asked Questions on the Phasedown of Hydrofluorocarbons (AIM Act)
records.authors_or_org: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency
records.claim_supported: Under the AIM Act, EPA is phasing down HFC production/consumption to 15% of baseline by 2036 via step targets (90% 2022-2023; 60% 2024-2028; 30% 2029-2033; 20% 2034-2035; 15% 2036+); ~75% of US HFC use (as of 2018) was in refrigeration/AC equipment; restrictions on higher-GWP HFCs in new refrigeration/AC/heat-pump equipment began January 1, 2025.
records.notes: Directly fetched this session. Directly load-bearing for any idea premised on refrigerant transition timing for two-phase/immersion cooling fluids or vapor-compression cooling equipment in the US. | Directly fetched this session (scout). records.title: 国家发展改革委 国家能源局 工业和信息化部 国家数据局印发《关于促进人工智能与能源双向赋能的行动方案》的通知 (Notice on the Action Plan for Promoting Two-Way Empowerment Between AI and Energy)
records.authors_or_org: National Development and Reform Commission; National Energy Administration; Ministry of Industry and Information Technology; National Data Administration (China)
records.claim_supported: Official joint notice (国能发科技〔2026〕34号, dated April 8, 2026) establishes a national AI-energy interaction action plan; references 'high-efficiency cooling' (高效冷却) as a research/application area and a green-power-direct-connection (绿电直连) framework, but does NOT contain an explicit quantified liquid-cooling mandate or PUE target in the notice text itself.
records.notes: IMPORTANT CONTRADICTION: multiple Chinese trade-press/secondary sources (e.g., searched summaries citing this policy) claim the plan mandates '100% liquid cooling for new large AI data centers' -- this specific claim was NOT found in the directly fetched primary notice text, which only mentions generic 'high-efficiency cooling' with no percentage mandate. Any idea or claim citing a '100% liquid cooling mandate' from this policy should be treated as unconfirmed/likely media overstatement pending a verifier's independent re-read of the full annexed action-plan document (only the notice page, not necessarily the full annex, was fetched this session). | PRESERVED CONTRADICTION (mission lane instruction 8): multiple Chinese trade-press/secondary sources claim this plan mandates '100% liquid cooling for new large AI data centers.' That specific claim was NOT found in the directly fetched primary notice text, which only mentions generic 'high-efficiency cooling' with no percentage mandate. This primary-notice record is kept authoritative; any record repeating the '100% mandate' claim elsewhere in this lane's ledger must be typed as secondary/unconfirmed, not averaged with this record. No such secondary record exists elsewhere in this raw ledger to re-type -- flag for downstream idea-architect/red-team review not to cite the 100% figure as confirmed.

records.title: Launch of second Data Centre - Call for Application (DC-CFA2)
records.authors_or_org: Singapore Economic Development Board (EDB) and Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA)
records.claim_supported: EDB and IMDA launched DC-CFA2 on December 1, 2025, allocating at least 200 MW of new data-center capacity; operators must be at least 50% powered via eligible green pathways, obtain BCA-IMDA Green Mark for Data Centres 2024 Platinum certification, achieve PUE <=1.25 at 100% IT load, and comply with SS 715:2025. A separate market-reporting figure (facilities above 30 MW must use liquid cooling for at least 20% of IT load) is NOT independently confirmed from the primary IMDA text.
records.notes: WebFetch of the IMDA page itself returned only page-structure/date-stamp content, not the full body text; the detailed PUE/green-power/certification requirements above are drawn from law-firm (KWM, Withers, Morgan Lewis) secondary summaries of the same official announcement, not independently confirmed from IMDA's full raw text this session. The '20% IT load must be liquid-cooled above 30MW' figure is from a distinct search-derived secondary source and was not corroborated in the fetched IMDA excerpt -- flag as moderately confirmed, not fully primary-verified. | Verifier re-attempted direct IMDA fetch this session; result was identical to scout's (nav/header only, JS-rendered). Accepted as a genuine T1 regulator/buyer-specification record given: (a) the core existence/date of DC-CFA2 is independently confirmed from the primary page itself, and (b) three independent professional law-firm client alerts corroborate the same quantitative requirements. The 20%-liquid-cooling-above-30MW figure specifically remains uncorroborated by the primary source and should be treated with more caution than the other cited figures.

records.title: Sandia's 'Cooler' technology offers fundamental breakthrough in heat transfer for microelectronics, other cooling applications
records.authors_or_org: Sandia National Laboratories
records.claim_supported: Sandia National Laboratories developed the 'Sandia Cooler' (Air Bearing Heat Exchanger), a rotating-fin air-cooling technology using centrifugal effect to thin the boundary layer ~10x, intended to reduce cooling energy for microelectronics/data centers; researchers estimated potential to decrease overall US electrical power consumption by >7% if broadly scaled. As of the July 2011 press release, Sandia was patent-pending and actively soliciting licensees (deadline July 15, 2011).
records.notes: Not independently fetched this session (search-snippet derived); press-release date (approx. 2012) is old relative to this lane's 2026-2031 trigger window and is included primarily as a national-lab primary-technology-development data point rather than a current demand signal. This is an old (2011) national-lab technology-development record, well outside the mission's 2026-2031 trigger window; retained as historical national-lab primary-technology data point, not a current demand signal. Verifier could not confirm current (2026) licensing/commercialization status from this dated press release.

records.title: Project Deschutes: A 2MW Coolant Distribution Unit (CDU) specification
records.authors_or_org: Google (presented via ARPA-E workshop; published through Open Compute Project)
records.claim_supported: Google published (and contributed to OCP) the Project Deschutes 2 MW coolant-distribution-unit (CDU) specification, requiring an aggressive 3C approach temperature difference (ATD), ~80 PSI available pressure, fully redundant per-pump-circuit power feeds, and 0.2-micron side-stream filtration; multiple cooling vendors (Boyd, CoolerMaster, Delta, Envicool, Nidec, nVent, Vertiv) have since built CDUs to this open specification.
records.notes: WebFetch retrieved the PDF binary but could not extract readable text this session; the technical figures above (3C ATD, 80 PSI, 0.2-micron filtration) are drawn from search-result summaries of vendor/trade coverage (Boyd, DCD, HPCwire) of the same specification, not independently re-derived from the raw slide text. This is a hyperscaler-originated buyer specification now functioning as a quasi-industry-standard -- a strong US buyer/procurement demand signal. | Accepted as a genuine hyperscaler buyer-specification (unlike L14-041/042, the underlying document was actually retrieved/confirmed to exist this session, just not text-parseable) -- a real US buyer/procurement demand signal now functioning as a quasi-industry-standard. All specific numeric figures should be treated as secondary-sourced/unconfirmed pending a rendering-capable re-fetch.

records.title: NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 rack-scale liquid-cooled system
records.authors_or_org: NVIDIA Corporation
records.claim_supported: NVIDIA states the GB200 NVL72 rack-scale system (36 Grace CPUs + 72 Blackwell GPUs) uses a rack-scale liquid-cooled design mandatory at this power density, claiming 25x more performance at the same power vs. H100 air-cooled infrastructure. Third-party technical commentary (not NVIDIA's own page) estimates ~72 kW of GPU heat alone per rack and >120 kW total rack heat, with recommended cold-plate flow of 2-3 L/min per module.
records.notes: Directly fetched this session. Precise per-GPU TDP (1000-1200W depending on source) and rack-level flow/pressure figures are third-party technical-press estimates (Introl, ToneCooling, SemiAnalysis, Alliance Chemical), not NVIDIA's own disclosed spec sheet numbers -- treat as vendor performance claim (T2) rather than an independently audited figure; flag the TDP discrepancy (1000W vs. 1200W per GPU across sources) as unresolved. | Directly fetched this session (scout). Per-GPU TDP and rack-level flow/pressure figures are third-party technical-press estimates (Introl, ToneCooling, SemiAnalysis, Alliance Chemical), not NVIDIA's own disclosed spec sheet -- correctly treated as a vendor performance claim (T2), not an independently audited figure; TDP discrepancy (1000W vs 1200W per GPU across sources) remains unresolved and should not be averaged.

records.title: Flex Acquires JetCool to Expand Data Center and Power Portfolio
records.authors_or_org: Flex Ltd. (NASDAQ: FLEX)
records.claim_supported: Flex (NASDAQ: FLEX) announced November 14, 2024 acquisition of JetCool Technologies, a microconvective direct-to-chip liquid-cooling company; deal financial terms not disclosed. JetCool's CDU handles racks up to 300 kW, scalable to over 2 MW in row-based configurations.
records.notes: Directly fetched via Flex's own investor-relations press release page this session. Deal value not disclosed by either party; do not infer a dollar figure. JetCool separately received a $1,265,747 DOE COOLERCHIPS subcontract in 2023 (see L14-030), a useful cross-link between government R&D funding and subsequent private M&A. | TIER DOWNGRADED from scout's T1 to T2: this is a company press release, not an audited financial filing (10-K/10-Q), so it does not meet SOURCE_STANDARDS' explicit T1 bar of 'audited company filing' -- it is still a directly-fetched, official, primary corporate source, hence T2 rather than lower. Applied consistently to all press-release-sourced 'company_filing' records in this ledger (see also L14-047, L14-048, L14-051). JetCool separately received a $1,265,747 DOE COOLERCHIPS subcontract in 2023 (L14-030).

records.title: ZutaCore Secures $100M+ Series C Funding to Accelerate AI Data Center Cooling Innovation
records.authors_or_org: ZutaCore, Inc.
records.claim_supported: ZutaCore raised $100M+ Series C funding (dated June 2, 2026) from investors including Mitsubishi Electric, Carrier Ventures, and Samsung Electronics (via Samsung Ventures), to scale its waterless, direct-to-chip, two-phase liquid-cooling platform; company states 75+ global deployments.
records.notes: Directly fetched this session from the company's own press-release blog (not an independent financial-press or SEC-type filing, hence T2 rather than T1). Strategic investors (Mitsubishi Electric, Samsung Electronics) are themselves major electronics/cooling-adjacent companies -- a useful corporate-strategic-interest signal for the Asia section of the brief. | Directly fetched this session (scout) from the company's own press-release blog. Strategic investors (Mitsubishi Electric, Samsung Electronics) are themselves major electronics/cooling-adjacent companies -- a useful corporate-strategic-interest signal for the Asia section.

records.title: Vertiv Holdings Co. FY2025 liquid-cooling order growth and backlog (10-K / investor disclosures)
records.authors_or_org: Vertiv Holdings Co. (NYSE: VRT)
records.claim_supported: Vertiv reported FY2025 total net sales of $10.23 billion (Q4 2025 net sales of $2.88 billion, up 23% YoY), Q4 2025 organic orders up approximately 252% YoY (trailing-twelve-month organic order growth approximately 81%), a Q4 2025 book-to-bill ratio of approximately 2.9x, and an order backlog of $15.0 billion entering 2026 (up 109% YoY); the company guides to $13.25-13.75 billion net sales for 2026 (27-29% organic growth). The release does not break out a liquid-cooling-specific order-growth percentage; that figure from the original raw record could not be corroborated and is dropped from this claim.
records.notes: Direct WebFetch of the SEC.gov 10-K URL returned HTTP 403 this session (consistent with SEC EDGAR bot-blocking seen in other lanes of this project); figures above are drawn from investor-press secondary aggregation of Vertiv's own reported/quoted figures, not independently re-derived from the primary filing text. Verifier should fetch the canonical 10-K directly (e.g., via an authenticated EDGAR tool) to confirm exact segment-level liquid-cooling revenue figures before treating any specific dollar amount as fully confirmed. | url/canonical_key retained as the original SEC EDGAR 10-K target (vrt-20251231.htm) for record-identity stability, even though that specific document remains unfetchable: sec.gov, data.sec.gov/submissions, sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar, and the EDGAR full-text-search endpoint all returned HTTP 403 again this session. The evidentiary basis for fetched=true is an official alternate hosting the same underlying disclosures -- Vertiv's own investor-relations press release (see locator) -- fetched directly this session. TIER/SOURCE_TYPE CORRECTED from the scout's original T1/company_filing to T2/market_industry per mission rule: the fetched document is a company-issued earnings press release, not audited 10-K filing text, so it is scored accordingly even though it is Vertiv's own official IR-hosted document. The unsupported '50%+ liquid-cooling order growth' figure from the original raw claim was removed because the fetched release does not disaggregate orders by product line; it can be re-added only if a future session finds it in a primary Vertiv disclosure (e.g., earnings call transcript or 10-K liquid-cooling segment commentary).

records.title: GS칼텍스, 데이터센터용 직접액체냉각유체 출시 (GS Caltex launches direct liquid cooling fluid for data centers)
records.authors_or_org: GS Caltex Corporation
records.claim_supported: GS Caltex launched 'Kixx DLC Fluid PG25' for direct-liquid-cooling of CPUs/GPUs on October 14, 2025; company previously supplied immersion-cooling fluid to Samsung SDS (2024) and demonstrated at LG U+'s Pyeongchon data center (2025), and partnered with Supermicro for AI-server thermal testing at its Daejeon research facility.
records.notes: Directly fetched this session from GS Caltex's own media hub (company-primary source). Korean-language local-primary source satisfying Asia local-language quota; names real named-customer relationships (Samsung SDS, LG U+, Supermicro) making it a useful demand-evidence record for the Korea section. | Directly fetched this session (scout) from GS Caltex's own media hub. records.title: 生成AI時代のデータセンター空調技術を結集させた検証施設 (Verification facility integrating generative-AI-era data center cooling technologies)
records.authors_or_org: NTT Facilities, Inc.
records.claim_supported: NTT Facilities opened its 'Products Engineering Hub for Data Center Cooling' within the NTT Musashino R&D Center, fully operational April 22, 2025, with simulated mock loads totaling 780 kW (420 kW air-cooled, 360 kW liquid-cooled), testing direct liquid cooling (CDU + stainless piping), chiller-less hybrid dry-cooler systems, and oil-free chillers.
records.notes: Directly fetched this session from NTT Facilities' own Japanese-language news page (company-primary source). Japanese-language local-primary source satisfying Asia local-language quota. A separate, related NTT initiative (a Data Center Trial Field in Chiba, opened Nov. 2024) and a Mitsubishi Heavy Industries/NTT Communications/NEC two-phase direct-chip-cooling demonstration were found via search but not independently fetched this session -- follow-up candidates for a future scout wave. | Directly fetched this session (scout) from NTT Facilities' own Japanese-language news page. Japanese-language local-primary source satisfying Asia local-language quota.

records.title: Trane Technologies Completes Acquisition of LiquidStack
records.authors_or_org: Trane Technologies plc / LiquidStack
records.claim_supported: Trane Technologies (publicly traded HVAC major) announced February 10, 2026 a definitive agreement to acquire two-phase-immersion-cooling vendor LiquidStack (Carrollton, TX; R&D also in Hong Kong), building on a 2023 minority investment; deal completed March 3, 2026; deal value not disclosed. LiquidStack will operate within Trane's Commercial HVAC business unit, Americas segment.
records.notes: UPGRADED this session: scout's original record cited only Tracxn (an investment-data aggregator), source_type trade_press. Verifier located and directly fetched Trane's own investor-relations press release, so source_type is corrected to company_filing (tier T2, consistent with the press-release standard applied elsewhere in this ledger, since this is not an audited filing). LiquidStack's last disclosed private funding round was $20M (Sept 2024) per the scout's original Tracxn note, not independently re-verified this session.

records.title: 工業技術與資訊》迎戰2,400瓦的散熱天塹 低壓冷媒兩相流冷板 揭開AI頂級算力封印 (Facing the 2,400W thermal barrier: low-pressure-refrigerant two-phase-flow cold plate unlocks top-tier AI compute)
records.authors_or_org: Industrial Technology Research Institute (工研院, ITRI), reported by Liberty Times (自由時報/自由評論網)
records.claim_supported: Taiwan's ITRI announced (article dated July 11, 2026) a low-pressure-refrigerant two-phase-flow cold-plate technology achieving single-chip heat dissipation of 2,400 W, phase-change heat removal 5-10x traditional single-phase liquid cooling, and >40% energy savings vs. conventional liquid cooling; ITRI is partnering with domestic cooling-supply-chain companies to build a complete two-phase cold-plate industrial chain, targeting compatibility with 45C warm-water cooling for next-gen platforms.
records.notes: Directly fetched this session; Traditional-Chinese-language Taiwan local-primary source satisfying Asia local-language quota. This is trade-press reporting of an ITRI (a government-affiliated, non-profit R&D institute akin to a national lab in Taiwan) announcement rather than ITRI's own primary release -- verifier should attempt to locate ITRI's own press release or technical paper to upgrade tier/source_type. Publication date (July 11, 2026) is one day before this scouting session, making this the most current record in the lane. | Directly fetched this session (scout); Traditional-Chinese-language Taiwan local-primary source satisfying Asia local-language quota. Verifier searched this session for an ITRI-own press release or technical paper to upgrade tier/source_type but found only ITRI's generic homepage, no specific primary release -- retained as trade_press T2 pending a future scout locating an ITRI-primary document.

records.title: Silicon Carbide Converters and MEMS Devices for High-temperature Power Electronics: A Critical Review
records.authors_or_org: Xiaorui Guo, Qian Xun, Zuxin Li, Shuxin Du (School of Engineering, Huzhou University, China)
records.claim_supported: SiC devices reach theoretical junction temperatures near 600C vs silicon's 150C ceiling; commercial SiC MOSFET/BJT/JFET devices operate reliably to ~210C with lab prototypes to 727C; four unresolved bottlenecks named: gate-drive design, high-temp current sensing, cross-temperature parameter matching, and packaging maturity.
records.notes: PMC full text fetched directly this session. Author affiliation (Huzhou University, China) confirmed via separate WebSearch, not the article page itself. | PMC full text fetched directly by scout; verifier independently confirmed title/journal/volume/issue/page/publisher/date via direct Crossref API lookup (works/10.3390/mi10060406) this session -- exact match. Peer review verified per MDPI Micromachines editorial policy. Author affiliation (Huzhou University, China) confirmed via separate WebSearch, not the article page itself.

records.title: Radiation Tolerance Testing Methodology of Robotic Manipulator Prior to Nuclear Waste Handling
records.authors_or_org: Kaiqiang Zhang, Chris Hutson, James Knighton, Guido Herrmann, Tom Scott (University of Bristol; University of Manchester)
records.claim_supported: A KUKA iiwa 7 LBR800 industrial robot arm failed after accumulating 164.55(+/-1.09) Gy of Co-60 gamma exposure, with failure in an optical encoder in the end effector; argues strategic use of standard (non-rad-hardened) components with planned replacement schedules can be more cost-effective than specialized rad-hard alternatives for some decommissioning tasks.
records.notes: UK-based authors; used as general technical-feasibility source for radiation-tolerant industrial control, not counted toward Asia mix. | PMC full text fetched by scout; verifier independently confirmed title/journal/volume/article-number/publisher/date via direct Crossref API lookup this session -- exact match. UK-based authors; used as general technical-feasibility source for radiation-tolerant industrial control, not counted toward Asia mix.

records.title: Prolonged silicon carbide integrated circuit operation in Venus surface atmospheric conditions
records.authors_or_org: Philip G. Neudeck, Roger D. Meredith, Liangyu Chen, David J. Spry, Leah M. Nakley, Gary W. Hunter (NASA Glenn Research Center)
records.claim_supported: A 3mm x 3mm 11-stage SiC JFET ring-oscillator IC chip was tested for prolonged operation in a ~460C, ~9.4 MPa caustic Venus-surface-simulated environment, demonstrating durability far beyond prior Venus-lander electronics (hours) -- part of the technical basis for NASA's HOTTech long-duration Venus surface mission concept.

records.title: 中子辐射导致的SiC功率器件漏电增加特性研究 (Study on characteristics of neutron-induced leakage current increase for SiC power devices)
records.authors_or_org: Peng Chao, Lei Zhi-Feng, Zhang Zhan-Gang, He Yu-Juan, Ma Teng, Cai Zong-Qi, Chen Yi-Qiang
records.claim_supported: 14 MeV neutron irradiation (fluence 1.18e11 cm^-2) of SiC Schottky barrier diodes and MOSFETs: SBD forward I-V is stable but reverse leakage increases significantly (deep-level defect at Ec-1.034 eV lowers Schottky barrier); MOSFET gate leakage increases ~3.3x at 15V gate voltage due to donor-type oxide defects -- direct evidence that SiC is not automatically radiation-immune even though it is more temperature-tolerant.
records.notes: Official CAS journal host (wulixb.iphy.ac.cn) fetched directly this session; title, authors, DOI, and article number confirmed from the article page itself. | Official CAS journal host (wulixb.iphy.ac.cn) fetched by scout. Verifier additionally confirmed this DOI DOES resolve directly via Crossref API this session (unlike the scout's bibliographic-search-query fallback) -- exact title/journal/volume/issue/article/authors/publisher match, upgrading evidence quality.

records.title: THERMAL: Temperature-Hardened Electronics for Reliable Mission-Critical Applications (DARPA SBIR solicitation DPA26BZ02-NV008)
records.authors_or_org: Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA)
records.claim_supported: DARPA solicits mixed-signal IC technology for reliable operation up to 800C using SiC/GaN. Phase I requires an amplifier with DC gain >20dB and unity-gain bandwidth >1MHz verified at 800C, plus a ring oscillator with propagation delay <500ns at 800C. Phase II requires an ADC stable at 800C, >10,000 samples/sec, 8-bit resolution, <1W power, 0-5V input range, SNR >40dB. Solicitation DPA26BZ02-NV008 closed June 24, 2026, under the DoW SBIR 2026 BAA.
records.notes: Page fetched directly this session. Demonstrates an active DoD-defined technical specification (800C, defined ADC performance) as a concrete buyer-side requirement, distinct from academic lab demonstrations. | Verifier independently re-fetched this page directly this session (200 OK, full readable content), confirming and expanding on the scout's original claim with additional Phase I/II numeric specs. Demonstrates a concrete DoD-defined technical buyer specification, distinct from academic lab demonstrations.

records.authors_or_org: U.S. Department of Energy, Geothermal Technologies Office / Utah FORGE
records.claim_supported: DOE awarded up to $49 million over three years (announced February 2021) across 17 projects under Solicitation 2020-1, including a topic area requiring devices/systems operational at temperatures up to 225C for zonal-isolation tools. Named awardees include Welltec, PetroQuip Energy Services, Colorado School of Mines, Battelle Memorial Institute, Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, Clemson University, Stanford University, Rice University, Fervo Energy, UT Austin, and Penn State.

Direct government funding-award evidence of buyer/procurement demand for 225C-rated downhole electronics. | Verifier independently re-fetched this page directly this session, confirming funding amount, project count, 225C technical requirement, and awardee list match the scout's original claim.

records.title: DOE FORGE R&D Solicitation 2022-2: reservoir stimulation, high-temperature proppants, and packer tool awards
records.authors_or_org: U.S. Department of Energy, Geothermal Technologies Office / Utah FORGE
records.claim_supported: DOE announced up to $44 million (August 2022) for 13 projects (selected/announced November 14, 2023) covering adaptive induced-seismicity monitoring, alternative stimulation schemes, field-scale heat-sweep efficiency, high-temperature proppants, and multiset straddle packers for open-hole operations. Named awardees include Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, NREL, Sandia National Laboratories, Oklahoma State University, Stevens Institute of Technology, PetroQuip, and Welltec.

records.title: SUPERHOT: Stimulate Utilization of Plentiful Energy in Rocks through High-temperature Original Technologies
records.authors_or_org: ARPA-E, U.S. Department of Energy
records.claim_supported: ARPA-E launched SUPERHOT on January 16, 2025 with $30 million to develop well-construction materials, testing facilities, and heat-extraction methods for reservoirs above 375C and 22 MPa, targeting 10-20 GW baseload potential and a 15-year well operational life; concept papers were due Feb 19, 2025, with awards anticipated November 2025.
